test_cases.test_case: Primary happy path - sign in with valid credentials (complete data)
preconditions: User exists in registered-user database with <email address> and <password>
steps: Navigate to the Login page
Enter <email address> in the Email/Username field
Enter <password> in the Password field
Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Minimum viable sign in using only required fields with an alternate account
preconditions: Alternate user exists in registered-user database with <alternate email address> and <alternate password>
steps: Navigate to the Login page
Enter <alternate email address> in the Email/Username field
Enter <alternate password> in the Password field
Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Alternative route: visit Forgot Password page then return and complete sign in
preconditions: User exists in registered-user database with <email address> and <password>
steps: Navigate to the Login page
Click the Forgot Password? link
Navigate back to the Login page
Enter <email address> in the Email/Username field
Enter <password> in the Password field
Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Forgot Password? link navigates to Forgot Password page
steps: Navigate to the Login page
Click the Forgot Password? link
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to Forgot Password page

test_cases.test_case: Sign in succeeds when Password meets complexity requirements
preconditions: User exists in registered-user database with <email address> and <password_with_uppercase_lowercase_number_and_special_character>
steps: Navigate to the Login page
Enter <email address> in the Email/Username field
Enter <password_with_uppercase_lowercase_number_and_special_character> in the Password field
Click the Sign In button
test_cases.expected_result: flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - complete data with 5+4 ZIP format
steps: Navigate to the Registration page
Enter <first name> in the First Name field
Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Select <state> from the State dropdown
Enter <ZIP Code in 5+4 format> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <SSN> in the SSN field
Enter <email address> in the Username field
Enter <password of at least 8 characters> in the Password field
Enter <same password as Password> in the Confirm Password field
Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Account created successfully — please sign in" and redirects to the Login Page

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - minimum viable required fields with 5-digit ZIP
steps: Navigate to the Registration page
Enter <first name> in the First Name field
Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Select <state> from the State dropdown
Enter <5-digit ZIP Code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <SSN> in the SSN field
Enter <email address> in the Username field
Enter <password of at least 8 characters> in the Password field
Enter <same password as Password> in the Confirm Password field
Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Account created successfully — please sign in" and redirects to the Login Page

test_cases.test_case: Alternative happy path - trigger phone and SSN auto-formatting before submit
steps: Navigate to the Registration page
Enter <first name> in the First Name field
Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Select <different state than in P-001> from the State dropdown
Enter <ZIP Code in 5+4 or 5-digit format> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <unformatted phone digits> in the Phone Number field
Enter <unformatted SSN digits> in the SSN field
Enter <email address> in the Username field
Enter <password of at least 8 characters> in the Password field
Enter <same password as Password> in the Confirm Password field
Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field displays formatted value in the (123) 456-7890 format and SSN field displays formatted value in the 123-45-6789 format before submit; after submit displays "Account created successfully — please sign in" and redirects to the Login Page

test_cases.test_case: State dropdown contains all US states and each option is selectable
steps: Navigate to the Registration page
Open the State dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: State dropdown contains the following options and each is selectable: Alabama, Alaska, Arizona, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, North Carolina, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Vermont, Virginia, Washington, West Virginia, Wisconsin, Wyoming

test_cases.test_case: Password boundary happy path - password exactly 8 characters
steps: Navigate to the Registration page
Enter <first name> in the First Name field
Enter <last name> in the Last Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Select <state> from the State dropdown
Enter <ZIP Code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <SSN> in the SSN field
Enter <email address> in the Username field
Enter <password of exactly 8 characters> in the Password field
Enter <same password as Password> in the Confirm Password field
Click the Register button
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Account created successfully — please sign in" and redirects to the Login Page

test_cases.test_case: Welcome message and Accounts table are displayed
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The page displays a welcome message that includes <user name> and the Accounts table is visible with columns for Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, and Open Date.

test_cases.test_case: All Accounts table columns are present
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The Accounts table shows column headers: Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, and Open Date as visible columns.

test_cases.test_case: Account numbers are masked for security
preconditions: At least one account exists with an account number
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: Each Account Number in the table is masked, showing only the last 4 digits in the format ****5001.

test_cases.test_case: Account Number displayed as a clickable link element
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The Account Number cell is rendered as a clickable link element for each row (Account Number column type is link as defined in the table AST).

test_cases.test_case: Clicking Account Number does not open account details (feature not implemented)
preconditions: Feature not implemented for Open Account action
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
Click the Account Number link in a row
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking the Account Number link does not navigate to account details or open an account details view; the UI remains on the Accounts Overview page because the Open Account feature is not implemented.

test_cases.test_case: Footer row displays total balance equal to sum of shown balances
preconditions: Multiple accounts exist with Current Balance values
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The table footer row is visible and displays a total balance value equal to the sum of the Current Balance values shown in all account rows.

test_cases.test_case: Rows are ordered by Open Date ascending (earliest first)
preconditions: At least two accounts exist with different <open dates>
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: Table rows are ordered by Open Date with the earliest Open Date in the first row (rows sorted ascending by Open Date as the default).

test_cases.test_case: Active Account Status badge is shown for accounts
preconditions: At least one account exists with Active status
steps: Navigate to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: Each account row with an active account displays an Account Status badge labeled "Active".

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - Complete data for Checking account
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount>
steps: Select Checking in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to accounts overview

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - Minimum viable required fields for Savings account
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount meeting Savings minimum>
steps: Select Savings in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to accounts overview

test_cases.test_case: Alternative route - switch account type after entering deposit and complete
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount meeting Savings minimum>
steps: Select Savings in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Select Checking in the Account Type radio options
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to accounts overview

test_cases.test_case: Account type options are present and selectable
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
steps: Click the Checking option in the Account Type radio options
Click the Savings option in the Account Type radio options
test_cases.expected_result: Radio options "Checking" and "Savings" are visible and can be selected

test_cases.test_case: Minimum deposit requirements display updates with account type selection
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
steps: Select Checking in the Account Type radio options
Select Savings in the Account Type radio options
test_cases.expected_result: The UI displays the Checking minimum deposit "$25" and the Savings minimum deposit "$100" as part of the account selection cards

test_cases.test_case: Real-time validation passes when deposit meets account minimum and funding balance
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount>
steps: Select Checking in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: No real-time validation error messages are visible for Account Type, Initial Deposit Amount, or Funding Source Account, and the Open Account button is enabled

test_cases.test_case: Funding source balance validated in real-time before submission
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount>
steps: Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time validation confirms the Funding Source Account balance is sufficient (no funding-related validation errors are shown)

test_cases.test_case: Successful submission redirects to accounts overview (redirect verification)
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount>
steps: Select Checking in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: After displaying "Account opened successfully!", the application navigates to the accounts overview page

test_cases.test_case: End-to-end lifecycle - open account using Savings path and confirm success message and redirect
preconditions: User on the Open New Account page
Funding source account <funding source account> exists with balance >= <initial deposit amount meeting Savings minimum>
steps: Select Savings in the Account Type radio options
Enter <initial deposit amount> in the Initial Deposit Amount field
Open the Funding Source Account dropdown
Select <funding source account> from the Funding Source Account dropdown
Click the Open Account button
test_cases.expected_result: "Account opened successfully!" is displayed and the user is redirected to accounts overview

test_cases.test_case: Internal transfer - complete data (My ParaBank Account)
preconditions: User has a Checking account and a Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select "My ParaBank Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Open the Destination Account dropdown
Select <destination account> from the Destination Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

test_cases.test_case: External transfer - complete data with matching account numbers
preconditions: User has a Checking account and a Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select "External Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Enter <external account number> in the External Account Number field
Enter <external account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

test_cases.test_case: Alternative route: switch from internal to external transfer and complete
preconditions: User has a Checking account and a Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select "My ParaBank Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Open the Destination Account dropdown
Select <destination account> from the Destination Account dropdown
Select "External Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <external account number> in the External Account Number field
Enter <external account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

test_cases.test_case: Transfer Type options present and conditional fields visibility
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Verify the radio option "My ParaBank Account" is present in the Transfer Type group
Verify the radio option "External Account" is present in the Transfer Type group
Select "My ParaBank Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Verify the Destination Account dropdown is visible
Select "External Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Verify the External Account Number field is visible
Verify the Confirm Account Number field is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer type options are present; selecting "My ParaBank Account" shows the internal Destination Account dropdown; selecting "External Account" shows External Account Number and Confirm Account Number fields

test_cases.test_case: Source Account dropdown is filtered to Checking and Savings accounts
preconditions: User has at least one Checking account and at least one Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Open the Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: All options in the Source Account dropdown are Checking or Savings accounts

test_cases.test_case: Internal transfer between two different ParaBank accounts completes successfully
preconditions: User has at least two ParaBank accounts (e.g., Checking and Savings)
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select "My ParaBank Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Open the Destination Account dropdown
Select a different <destination account> from the Destination Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

test_cases.test_case: External transfer requires matching account numbers and completes when they match
preconditions: User has a Checking account or a Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select "External Account" in the Transfer Type radio group
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Enter <external account number> in the External Account Number field
Enter the same <external account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

test_cases.test_case: Successful transfer displays success message and transaction ID
preconditions: User has a Checking account or a Savings account
steps: Navigate to the Transfer page
Select a valid Transfer Type
Enter <transfer amount> in the Transfer Amount field
Open the Source Account dropdown
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
If Transfer Type is "My ParaBank Account", select <destination account> from the Destination Account dropdown
If Transfer Type is "External Account", enter <external account number> in the External Account Number field and enter the same <external account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Transfer completed successfully and returns a transaction ID

Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field

Click the Pay button

Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field

Click the Pay button

test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully. Returns a reference code and updates account balances.

test_cases.test_case: Happy path — payment amount equals available funds (pay full available balance)

steps: Navigate to the Bill Payment page
Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Enter <state> in the State field
Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Click the Pay button

test_cases.test_case: End-to-end lifecycle — submit payment and verify visible reference code and balance update
steps: Navigate to the Bill Payment page
Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Enter <state> in the State field
Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Enter <payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully. Returns a reference code and updates account balances. A payment confirmation containing a reference code is visible and the account balance for <source account> reflects the deducted <payment amount>.

test_cases.test_case: Lifecycle — make two sequential valid payments from the same Source Account and verify balances update after each
preconditions: <source account> has available funds >= <first payment amount> + <second payment amount>
steps: Navigate to the Bill Payment page
Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Enter <state> in the State field
Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Enter <first payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Click the Pay button
Enter <payee name> in the Payee Name field
Enter <street address> in the Street Address field
Enter <city> in the City field
Enter <state> in the State field
Enter <zip code> in the ZIP Code field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Payee Account Number field
Enter <payee account number> in the Confirm Account Number field
Enter <second payment amount> in the Payment Amount field
Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Payment submitted successfully. Returns a reference code and updates account balances. Both payments complete successfully, each returns a reference code, and the balance of <source account> is reduced first by <first payment amount> and then by <second payment amount>.

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - Complete data (Personal) with required down payment and sufficient collateral
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance >= 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Click the Personal loan type card
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Enter <down payment amount at least 10% of the loan amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance >= 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Happy path - Minimum required fields only (Auto) without Down Payment
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance >= 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Click the Auto loan type card
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance >= 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Alternative route - change Loan Type after entering amount and submit (edit flow)
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance >= 20% of the final chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Click the Home loan type card
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Enter <down payment amount at least 10% of the loan amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
Click the Auto loan type card
Enter <updated loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance >= 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Loan Type options are present and selectable
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
test_cases.expected_result: The Loan Type options 'Personal', 'Auto', and 'Home' are visible and selectable.

test_cases.test_case: Collateral selection with balance greater than required 20% enables approval
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance greater than 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Select <loan type> from the Loan Type options
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance greater than 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Collateral selection with balance equal to exact 20% required collateral results in approval
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance equal to 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Select <loan type> from the Loan Type options
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance equal to 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Primary lifecycle - request loan and verify approval displays account details
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance >= 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Select <loan type> from the Loan Type options
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
If available, enter <down payment amount at least 10% of the loan amount> in the Down Payment Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance >= 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: Loan approved and created successfully! Displays account details.

test_cases.test_case: Alternative lifecycle - denial due to inadequate collateral value
preconditions: User has at least one collateral account with balance less than 20% of the chosen loan amount
steps: Navigate to the Request Loan page
Select <loan type> from the Loan Type options
Enter <loan amount within the valid range for the selected Loan Type> in the Loan Amount field
Select <collateral account with balance less than 20% of the loan amount> from the Collateral Account dropdown
Click the Submit button
test_cases.expected_result: The application is denied and displays the reason 'Inadequate collateral value'.

test_cases.test_case: Card Controls Form - Path A (complete data) updates controls successfully
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Enter <numeric limit within policy> in the New Spending Limit field
Click Add Travel Notice
Enter <start date> in the Start Date field for the first Travel Notice item
Enter <end date> in the End Date field for the first Travel Notice item
Enter <destination> in the Destination field for the first Travel Notice item
Select 'Frozen' from the Card Status dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Card Controls Form - Path B (minimum viable) updates using only required field
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Card Controls Form - Path C (alternate route: add travel notice) updates successfully
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Click Add Travel Notice
Enter <start date> in the Start Date field for the Travel Notice item
Enter <end date> in the End Date field for the Travel Notice item
Enter <destination> in the Destination field for the Travel Notice item
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Card Status transition: change card from Active to Frozen
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Select 'Frozen' from the Card Status dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Travel Notice with valid date range is accepted
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Click Add Travel Notice
Enter <start date> in the Start Date field where Start_Date is on or before End_Date
Enter <end date> in the End Date field where End_Date is on or after Start_Date
Enter <destination> in the Destination field for the Travel Notice item
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Multiple Travel Notices can be added and updated successfully
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Click Add Travel Notice
Enter <start date> in the Start Date field for the first Travel Notice item
Enter <end date> in the End Date field for the first Travel Notice item
Enter <destination> in the Destination field for the first Travel Notice item
Click Add Travel Notice
Enter <start date> in the Start Date field for the second Travel Notice item
Enter <end date> in the End Date field for the second Travel Notice item
Enter <destination> in the Destination field for the second Travel Notice item
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Card Status transition: change card from Frozen back to Active
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Select 'Active' from the Card Status dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Card Type dropdown contains and allows selection of 'Debit' and 'Credit'
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Request form
Open the Card Type dropdown
Select 'Debit' from the Card Type dropdown
Open the Card Type dropdown
Select 'Credit' from the Card Type dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Both 'Debit' and 'Credit' are present in the Card Type dropdown and each can be selected.

test_cases.test_case: Card Status dropdown contains and allows selection of 'Active' and 'Frozen'
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Open the Card Status dropdown
Select 'Active' from the Card Status dropdown
Open the Card Status dropdown
Select 'Frozen' from the Card Status dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Both 'Active' and 'Frozen' are present in the Card Status dropdown and each can be selected.

test_cases.test_case: End-to-end: submit a card request then update controls for an existing card
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Request form
Select 'Debit' from the Card Type dropdown
Select <account in good standing> in the Account to Link field
Click the Request Card button
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Enter <numeric limit within policy> in the New Spending Limit field
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: First, a card-request ticket is opened and the page shows "Card request submitted successfully." with a tracking ID. Then, "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Full lifecycle: change card status Active -> Frozen -> Active
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Select 'Frozen' from the Card Status dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Select 'Active' from the Card Status dropdown
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: After the first update, "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed. After the second update, "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Update New Spending Limit within policy succeeds
preconditions: At least one <existing card> is present
steps: Navigate to the Manage Cards page
Open the Card Controls form
Select <existing card> from the Select Existing Card dropdown
Enter <numeric limit within policy> in the New Spending Limit field
Click the Update Controls button
test_cases.expected_result: "Card controls updated successfully." is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Trade Funds Form - Complete Buy trade (all fields filled)
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Select Buy from the Action dropdown
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <quantity> in the Quantity field
Select <funding account> from the Funding or Destination Account dropdown
Click the Execute Trade button
test_cases.expected_result: executes same-day trade, updates holdings, displays "Trade executed successfully." with order ID

test_cases.test_case: Trade Funds Form - Minimum viable Buy trade (only required fields)
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Select Buy from the Action dropdown
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <quantity> in the Quantity field
Select <funding account> from the Funding or Destination Account dropdown
Click the Execute Trade button
test_cases.expected_result: executes same-day trade, updates holdings, displays "Trade executed successfully." with order ID

test_cases.test_case: Trade Funds Form - Alternative route: Sell trade with sufficient share balance
preconditions: Customer has sufficient share balance for the selected fund
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Select Sell from the Action dropdown
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <quantity> in the Quantity field
Select <destination account> from the Funding or Destination Account dropdown
Click the Execute Trade button
test_cases.expected_result: executes same-day trade, updates holdings, displays "Trade executed successfully." with order ID

test_cases.test_case: Recurring Investment Plan Form - Complete Weekly plan (all fields filled)
preconditions: Funding account <funding account> has adequate balance for initial contribution
Contribution amount meets minimum contribution amount
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <contribution amount> in the Contribution Amount field
Select Weekly from the Frequency dropdown
Enter <future date> in the Start Date field
Select <funding account> from the Funding Account dropdown
Click the Create Plan button
test_cases.expected_result: stores schedule and shows "Plan created successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Recurring Investment Plan Form - Minimum viable plan (only required fields)
preconditions: Funding account <funding account> has adequate balance for initial contribution
Contribution amount meets minimum contribution amount
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <contribution amount> in the Contribution Amount field
Select Monthly from the Frequency dropdown
Enter <future date> in the Start Date field
Select <funding account> from the Funding Account dropdown
Click the Create Plan button
test_cases.expected_result: stores schedule and shows "Plan created successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Recurring Investment Plan Form - Alternative route: Monthly frequency selection
preconditions: Funding account <funding account> has adequate balance for initial contribution
Contribution amount meets minimum contribution amount
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Enter <existing fund symbol> in the Fund Symbol field
Enter <contribution amount> in the Contribution Amount field
Select Monthly from the Frequency dropdown
Enter <future date> in the Start Date field
Select <funding account> from the Funding Account dropdown
Click the Create Plan button
test_cases.expected_result: stores schedule and shows "Plan created successfully."

test_cases.test_case: Verify Action dropdown contains Buy and Sell options
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Open the Action dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Action dropdown displays the options "Buy" and "Sell" and each option is selectable

test_cases.test_case: Verify Frequency dropdown contains Weekly and Monthly options
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Open the Frequency dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Frequency dropdown displays the options "Weekly" and "Monthly" and each option is selectable

test_cases.test_case: Fund Symbol search/autocomplete in Trade Funds Form selects an existing symbol
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
Focus the Fund Symbol search field in the Trade Funds Form
Enter <existing fund symbol> into the Fund Symbol search field
Select the matching entry from the autocomplete suggestions
test_cases.expected_result: Fund Symbol field is populated with the selected symbol from the suggestions

test_cases.test_case: Portfolio snapshot is visible and read-only
steps: Navigate to the Investments page
test_cases.expected_result: Portfolio snapshot panel is visible and displays current fund holdings, market value, and unrealised gain or loss and is read-only

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement using Month-and-year with Account selected (complete data)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Select "Month-and-year" in the Statement_Period radio
Enter <month-and-year> in the Month field
Select <account> from the Account dropdown
Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully; retrieves relevant transactions

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement using Month-and-year with only required fields (minimum viable)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Select "Month-and-year" in the Statement_Period radio
Enter <month-and-year> in the Month field
Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully; retrieves relevant transactions

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement using Custom date range with Account selected (alternative route)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Select "Custom date range" in the Statement_Period radio
Enter <start date> in the Start_Date field
Enter <end date> in the End_Date field
Select <account> from the Account dropdown
Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully; retrieves relevant transactions

test_cases.test_case: Enable paperless and save preference with valid email (complete data)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Check the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox
Enter <valid email address> in the Email_Address field
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: e-Statement preference updated.

test_cases.test_case: Save preference with Paperless_Opt_In left unchecked (minimum viable)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Ensure the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox is unchecked
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: e-Statement preference updated.

test_cases.test_case: Toggle paperless preference both directions: opt-in then opt-out (bidirectional)
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Check the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox
Enter <valid email address> in the Email_Address field
Click the Save Preference button
Uncheck the Paperless_Opt_In checkbox
Click the Save Preference button
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays "e-Statement preference updated." after the first Save Preference; after unchecking and clicking Save Preference again the system displays "e-Statement preference updated." again.

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement for a single-day custom range where Start_Date equals End_Date
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Select "Custom date range" in the Statement_Period radio
Enter <start date> in the Start_Date field
Enter the same <start date> in the End_Date field
Click the Generate Statement button
test_cases.expected_result: Statement generated successfully; retrieves relevant transactions

test_cases.test_case: Both forms are visible side by side on the Statements page
steps: Navigate to the Statements page
Verify the Generate Statement form is visible
Verify the E-Statement Preference form is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Generate Statement form is visible and E-Statement Preference form is visible, displayed side by side

test_cases.test_case: Change password with complete valid data (happy path - complete data)
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new password> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: The UI displays the message "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated.

test_cases.test_case: Change password using only required fields (happy path - minimum viable)
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new password> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: The UI displays the message "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated.

test_cases.test_case: Change password using an alternative valid strong password (happy path - alternative route)
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <different valid strong password> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching different valid strong password> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: The UI displays the message "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated.

test_cases.test_case: Collapsible panel reveals and hides the change password form
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Click to expand the Security Settings panel
Verify the Current Password field is visible
Verify the New Password field is visible
Verify the Confirm New Password field is visible
Verify the "Change Password" button is visible
Click to collapse the Security Settings panel
test_cases.expected_result: Expanding the Security Settings panel reveals the Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password fields and the "Change Password" button. Collapsing the panel hides these elements.

test_cases.test_case: Sequential password changes to confirm credentials are updated (end-to-end lifecycle)
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password A> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new strong password A> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
Enter <new strong password A> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password B> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new strong password B> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: After the first submission the UI displays "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated. After the second submission the UI again displays "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated, demonstrating the updated credentials were accepted.

test_cases.test_case: Change password with matching confirmation (positive confirmation match check)
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new password> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: The UI displays the message "Password changed successfully." and credentials are updated, confirming the Confirm New Password matched the New Password.

test_cases.test_case: Success message is shown after valid change password submission
steps: Navigate to the Security Settings page
Expand the Security Settings panel
Enter <current password> in the Current Password field
Enter <new strong password> in the New Password field
Enter <confirm new password matching new password> in the Confirm New Password field
Click the "Change Password" button
test_cases.expected_result: The success message "Password changed successfully." is displayed on the page after submission.

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message - Path A (Complete data: all fields filled including optional attachment and subject)
steps: Enter <subject> in the Subject field
Select <category> from the Category dropdown
Enter <message body> in the Message Body field
Upload <valid file> to the Attachment field
Click the Send Message button
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message - Path B (Minimum viable: only required Message Body provided)
steps: Enter <message body> in the Message Body field
Click the Send Message button
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Secure Message - Path C (Alternative route: select a different category and include optional attachment)
steps: Enter <message body> in the Message Body field
Select <alternative category> from the Category dropdown
Upload <valid file> to the Attachment field
Click the Send Message button
test_cases.expected_result: Message sent successfully with ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Category dropdown - verify listed options are present and selectable
steps: Open the Category dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Category dropdown shows options: Account, Technical, Security, Other and each option is selectable

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback - Path A (Complete data: all fields filled including optional Reason and Time Window, edit phone if desired)
steps: Select <reason for call> from the Reason for Call dropdown
Enter <preferred date on or after the next business day> in the Preferred Date field
Enter <preferred time window> in the Preferred Time Window field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Click the Request Callback button
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and sends an email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback - Path B (Minimum viable: only required fields provided; phone number is pre-filled)
steps: Enter <preferred date on or after the next business day> in the Preferred Date field
Click the Request Callback button
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and sends an email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback - Path C (Alternative route: edit pre-filled phone number and select a Reason for Call)
steps: Select <reason for call> from the Reason for Call dropdown
Enter <preferred date on or after the next business day> in the Preferred Date field
Enter <preferred time window> in the Preferred Time Window field
Enter <phone number> in the Phone Number field
Click the Request Callback button
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and sends an email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Schedule Callback - Preferred Date boundary accepted (use the next business day)
steps: Enter <preferred date equal to the next business day> in the Preferred Date field
Click the Request Callback button
test_cases.expected_result: Callback request submitted and sends an email confirmation

test_cases.test_case: Phone Number field pre-population - verify phone number is pre-filled and editable
steps: Focus the Phone Number field
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field displays a pre-filled phone number value and the value is editable
